campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a public hearing on July 7, 2026, to consider revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for two smoke shops in the city. The council also discussed adopting a new interim ordinance related to Senate legislation and approving consultant service agreements for a public works grant submission.
keyTopics: Revoking business licenses for California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop
New interim ordinance implementing Senate legislation
Public Works grant submission authorization
Consultant service agreements approval

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to approve financial reports and move forward with an ordinance that will be formally adopted later in May. The council also discussed whether the city should become a charter city and reviewed potential tax options and revenue estimates.
keyTopics: Preliminary approval of the Engineer's Report for fiscal year 2026
New ordinance scheduled for final adoption on May 20, 2026
Public hearing conducted on a resolution of intention
Charter city status—council exploring benefits and providing direction to staff
Preliminary tax options and revenue estimates presented for consideration

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve financial reports, authorize several construction and service contracts, and hear public appeals on unspecified matters following a court order.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Approved three-year professional services agreement
Heard public appeals on matters stemming from court order
Reviewed and approved minutes from prior closed session meeting

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several major items including designating a historic building at 647 South Sixth Street as a city landmark, approving a long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy, and considering a change to how the city elects mayors and council members in vacant positions. The council also took action on advertising signs at San José airport.
keyTopics: Historic landmark designation and Mills Act contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Proposed ballot measure to allow ranked choice voting for filling mayor and council vacancies
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America
Large format advertising sign contract at San José Mineta International Airport

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, to discuss a new residential development project at 333 Franklin Street, appoint a new City Attorney, and address ongoing legal matters involving the city.
keyTopics: Residential development project planned for 333 Franklin Street
City Attorney position appointment
Ongoing lawsuit: San Francisco Baykeeper v. City of Mountain View and City of Sunnyvale (federal court case)
Anticipated litigation discussion with legal counsel

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 p.m. The closed session portion means specific discussion topics were not disclosed to the public, which is standard practice for certain personnel, legal, or confidential matters.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting
Regular council meeting followed at 7 p.m.
Flag salute and public session convening

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a study session to review and update the Health and Safety section of the city's long-term General Plan (covering 2015-2040). The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for its work across multiple program areas during Parks and Recreation Month, and honored Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad Team for its achievement.
keyTopics: Health and Safety Element update for the General Plan
Parks and Recreation Department recognition and programs
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — July 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on July 16, 2026, included a disclosure about legal time limits for residents who want to challenge city decisions. If the city makes a quasi-adjudicative decision (a decision that affects individual rights), you have 90 days from the final decision to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, following state law.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over quasi-adjudicative decisions
Legal time limits governed by California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve budget planning documents for federal housing and community development funding, reviewed requests from council members for future work items, and discussed changes to how the city fills council vacancies. The meeting also included routine approvals of meeting minutes and calendars.
keyTopics: Federal housing and community development budget amendment for 2025-2030
Changes to the council vacancy process through a new ordinance
Council member requests for future agenda items and city projects
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council honored Bill Gutoff's retirement and held a public hearing on utility rate changes for water, wastewater, and trash services in fiscal year 2027. The Finance Committee recommended approving new rate schedules for all three utilities.
keyTopics: Water rate increases and reserve management for FY 2027
Wastewater collection rate changes and reserve practices
Trash/refuse utility rate adjustments
Bill Gutoff retirement recognition
